outcome.reason: Observation is now PROVEN and consistent, but attribution to candidate defb5506 remains UNPROVEN under the strict doctrine (deployed SHA == candidate SHA): production serves e0165192, a descendant. The earlier observer disagreement is explained: the old-content fetches predated the first successful deploy (run 11, 07:18-07:21Z) — runs 2-9 of the production workflow FAILED, so live content was stale until 07:21Z; apex (308 redirect, no body) vs www probing differences add divergence for non-redirect-following observers.

attribution.reason: Deployed production SHA e0165192cd625366bb95a8f682db14e1facff58f != candidate_sha defb5506677d4f5b88178b027b7ba6604ea47c42, and registry doctrine requires equality. Facts: candidate IS an ancestor of the deployed release; the live content differs from the candidate's tree by exactly one character in the homepage h1 (em-dash removed by the founder's own PR-head refinement 61ea44f2, 'Refine AI Motor homepage headline') plus zero /motors/ delta; live content is byte-identical to the founder-APPROVED merge a80f828f. Strict equality to defb5506 is permanently unsatisfiable because production deploys main merge commits, never mid-PR commits — closing requires a founder/doctrine decision: re-candidate the job artifact to the approved merge (or PR head), or accept the record can never reach RECEIPT_COMPLETE. Not forced (governed-autorun: a failing job fixes reality, never the verifier).
